title: App.css - Global Stylesheet
content:
This file defines the global CSS variables and base styles for the DocMed application.

## Purpose

- **CSS Variables:** Establishes a consistent color palette and layout dimensions (e.g., sidebar width, border radius) for the entire application.
- **Base Styles:** Sets fundamental styles for the `body` element, including font, background, and text color.
- **Layout:** Defines the basic flexbox structure for the main layout, separating the sidebar and the main content area.
- **Component Styling:** Provides foundational styles for various UI components like the tab bar, sidebar, navigation, file tree, markdown rendering, and utility elements.

## Key Styles and Components

### Color Palette & Variables

- `--bg`, `--surface`, `--surface-2`: Background and surface colors.
- `--border`, `--border-2`: Border colors.
- `--text`, `--text-2`, `--text-3`: Text colors.
- `--accent`, `--accent-dim`: Accent colors for highlights and active states.
- `--sidebar-w`: Width of the sidebar.
- `--radius`: Border radius for elements.

### Layout (`.layout`, `.main-column`)

- Implements a flexbox layout for the main application structure.

### Tab Bar (`.tab-bar`, `.tab-list`, `.tab`, `.tab-close`, `.tab-add`)

- Styles for the horizontal tab interface used for navigating documents.
- Includes active tab highlighting and close/add button styling.

### Sidebar (`.sidebar`, `.sidebar.collapsed`, `.sidebar-top`, `.repo-title`, `.collapse-btn`, `.sidebar-body`)

- Styles the collapsible sidebar, including its top section (repository title, collapse button) and body.

### Search (`.search-wrap`, `.search-icon`, `.search-input`, `.search-clear`)

- Styles for the search input field within the sidebar.

### Navigation (`.nav`, `.nav-item`, `.nav-badge`, `.nav-section-label`, `.nav-empty`)

- Styles for the navigation menu within the sidebar, including active states and section labels.

### File Tree (`.tree-level`, `.tree-row`, `.tree-folder-row`, `.tree-chevron`, `.tree-icon`, `.tree-name`, `.tree-children`)

- Styles for the hierarchical file tree component.

### Main Content (`.main`, `.page`, `.page-title`, `.page-subtitle`)

- Styles for the primary content area where documents are displayed.

### Stats (`.stats-row`, `.stat-card`, `.stat-value`, `.stat-label`)

- Styles for displaying statistical information.

### Home Sections (`.section-title`, `.file-grid`, `.file-card`, `.file-card-name`, `.file-card-path`, `.file-card-title`)

- Styles for sections on the home page, including file grids and cards.

### Commits (`.commit-list`, `.commit-item`, `.commit-sha`, `.commit-body`, `.commit-msg`, `.commit-meta`)

- Styles for displaying commit history.

### Doc Page (`.doc-filepath`)

- Styles for the file path display on documentation pages.

### Markdown (`.markdown-body`)

- Styles for rendering markdown content, including headings, paragraphs, links, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, and tables.

### Misc (`.empty-state`, `.loading-pulse`)

- Styles for empty states and loading animations.

## Usage

This stylesheet is imported into the main `App.js` (or equivalent entry point) and its styles are applied globally across the application. Developers can extend or override these styles by creating new CSS classes or using CSS-in-JS solutions that leverage these global variables.

```css
/* Example of using a CSS variable */
.my-component {
  background-color: var(--surface);
  color: var(--text-2);
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
}

/* Example of overriding a style */
.page-title {
  font-size: 2rem; /* Overrides the default 1.75rem */
}
```
